info.title: Product Indications
info.description: API to get HDB product indications & locarno classifications

get.description:
Retrieves Locarno class headings

The Locarno Classification headings describe in very general terms the nature of the different product indications classified under them.

headings.heading: Bakers' products, biscuits, pastry, pasta and other cereal products, chocolates, confectionery, ices

get.description:
Gets Taxonomy tree

The Taxonomy is based on the 45 Class Headings of the Nice Classification (34 for Goods and 11 for Services). 
Each Nice class is divided into groups of terms of similar characteristics and the groups ordered within a hierarchy 
ranging from the more general to the more specific. Each grouping has a “Group title” that is coherent with the 
current market situation and which bears reference to the relevant Nice Class Heading.

items.text: Bakers' products, biscuits, pastry, pasta and other cereal products, chocolates, confectionery, ices
Fruit, vegetables and products made from fruits and vegetables
Cheeses, butter and butter substitutes, other dairy produce

get.description: Retrieves a list of product indications that match specified criteria

200.description: Example HDB term list response that meets the given specified criteria

post.description:
Checks every term against the harmonised DB and against validation rules for invalid characters, syntax, max
term length, duplicate terms, etc.

requestBody.description: Example of list of terms to be checked

errors.title: Suggested term
errors.detail: Suggested term: classNumber '19', subClassNumber '8', conceptId '12159817' and text 'Access cards'

Oauth2ClientCredentials.description:
- **Client Credentials Flow:** In this method, the final user remains anonymous. The API authenticates the client 
based on their credentials but does not have access to the user’s identity.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: The maximum number of requests you're permitted to make per time period.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The relative time in seconds until the current rate-limit window resets.

Retry-After.description: The number of seconds to wait until the rate limit window resets. Only sent when the rate limit is reached.

ParentConceptId.name: parentConceptId
ParentConceptId.description: Concept identifier of the parent node in taxonomy

PageNumber.name: page
PageNumber.description: Number of the page when paginating (starts with 0)

PageSize.name: size
PageSize.description: Size of the page when paginating. Values between 10 and 250.

UnauthorizedError.description: Client request has not been completed because it lacks valid authentication credentials for the requested resource

example.title: The request lacks valid authentication credentials for this resource
example.detail: Full authentication is required to access this resource

DefaultError.description: Error occurred - see status code and problem object for more information.

PageNumber.description: The results are returned in pages. The page parameter (zero-based) indicates which page to return. For example, page = 2 (third page) and size = 10 will return matching applications No 21 to No 30

PageSize.description: Number of results to fetch per page

ClassNumber.description: Locarno class number. Allowed values between 1 and 32.

SubClassNumber.description: Locarno sub class number. Allowed values between 0 and 99.

Level.description: Level in the taxonomy tree. It starts from 0 and increments from 1 to 1

terms.description: List of terms that meet the search criteria

sourceLanguage.description: Language used in the product indication classification
Language used in the product indication classification

productIndications.description: Classification validation result detailed by product indication

A short summary of the problem type. Written in English and readable
for engineers, usually not suited for non technical stakeholders and
not localized.

problem that is helpful to locate the problem and give advice on how
to proceed. Written in English and readable for engineers, usually
not suited for non technical stakeholders and not localized.

example.title: Max number of terms exceeded
example.detail: Your list contains more than 5 terms. A wide range of product indications can increase the probability of a design being opposed or possibly cancelled due to the conflict with an earlier design with similar product indications, or non-use of these. Are you sure you need all the product indications in your list?

type.description:
A URI reference that uniquely identifies the problem type only in
the context of the provided API. Opposed to the specification in
RFC-7807, it is neither recommended to be dereferencable and point
to a human-readable documentation nor globally unique for the
problem type.

occurrence of the problem.

A human readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the
problem that is helpful to locate the problem and give advice on how
to proceed. Written in English and readable for engineers, usually

instance.description:
A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the
problem, e.g. by adding a fragment identifier or sub-path to the
problem type. May be used to locate the root of this problem in the
source code.
